item.title: 🇺🇳 US and Bahrain push UN resolution for Hormuz maritime coalition
item.description: UN Security Council members begin talks on a US-Bahrain backed draft resolution that could authorize a multinational maritime coalition to secure the Strait of Hormuz amid Iran's effective closure of the waterway.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇨🇳 Iran foreign minister to visit China ahead of Trump trip
item.description: Iran's Abbas Araghchi will meet Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi in Beijing before Donald Trump's scheduled visit, as Tehran seeks allies amid escalating tensions with the US over the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: 🇷🇴 Romanian prime minister ousted in no-confidence vote
item.description: Romania's pro-EU government fell after parliament passed a no-confidence motion initiated by Social Democrats and far-right parties, deepening political instability in the NATO member state.

item.title: 🛢️ Iraq offers deep discounts for tankers braving Hormuz
item.description: Iraq has offered to cut crude oil prices by more than 25% for shipping firms willing to risk transit through the Strait of Hormuz, as hundreds of ships remain stranded off Dubai.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkish company signs deal to export 100,000 kamikaze drones
item.description: Turkish defense firm Pasifik Teknoloji announced a major agreement to sell tens of thousands of FPV kamikaze drones, highlighting Turkey's growing defense export industry.

item.title: ⚔️ US Sinks Iranian Boats as Tehran Attacks UAE and Strait Shipping
item.description: Iran launched attacks on UAE and tankers in the Strait of Hormuz while the US destroyed Iranian boats attempting to interfere with "Project Freedom." The fragile ceasefire continues to unravel as both sides exchange fire.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Two Russian Hypersonic Scientists Convicted of Treason
item.description: Two Russian physicists involved in hypersonic missile research were sentenced to 12.5 years each in a penal colony for treason, the latest in a series of prosecutions targeting scientists working on sensitive weapons programs.

item.title: 🇰🇵 North Korean Sports Team to Visit South Korea for First Time Since 2018
item.description: North Korea's Naegohyang Women's FC will play in South Korea, marking the first sports team visit from the North since 2018. Analysts caution the match does not signal a broader diplomatic thaw.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 Trump Looks Forward to Xi Summit as US-China Tensions Mount
item.description: President Trump said he looks forward to meeting China's Xi Jinping later this month, calling the summit "very important" amid tensions over trade, technology, and the Iran war that has disrupted energy flows.

item.title: 🇪🇺 NATO Chief Says Europeans "Got the Message" on Defense After Trump Drawdown
item.description: European leaders are reassessing their role in NATO after Trump announced the withdrawal of 5,000 US troops from Germany, with the alliance chief saying Europeans are "stepping up" on defense responsibility.

item.title: ⚔️ Middle East Ceasefire in Doubt as US and Iran Battle for Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran was under strain after both sides exchanged fire in the Gulf. Iran warned the fight over the strategic waterway has not even begun yet, while the US destroyed six Iranian small boats during escort operations.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump's Germany Troop Cuts Show Limits of NATO Efforts to Keep US on Board
item.description: European officials have been working to convince Trump to keep the US in NATO despite tensions over the Iran war, but his abrupt move to cut US forces in Germany signals such efforts have limits.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia Declares Victory Day Ceasefire in Ukraine, Kyiv Announces Earlier Truce
item.description: Russia declared a unilateral ceasefire for May 8-9 to mark Victory Day, while Ukraine announced its own ceasefire two days earlier. Zelenskyy called Russian attacks during ceasefire talk "utter cynicism."

item.title: 🇦🇲 Armenia Hosts Historic EU Summit as It Charts Course Away from Russia
item.description: Armenia hosted its first EU summit in Yerevan, marking a deepening of ties with Europe. French President Macron highlighted the country's shift away from Russian influence amid tensions with Moscow.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Sanctions Russian Recruitment of Migrants to Fight Against Ukraine
item.description: Britain unveiled sanctions against 35 people and entities involved in recruiting vulnerable migrants to fight for Russia against Ukraine and producing drones for the conflict.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Signals Readiness to Export J-35A Fighter Jet to Pakistan
item.description: State media showed a J-35A model with AVIC logo and English lettering, suggesting China's first export of its new stealth fighter may go to Pakistan amid growing defense ties.

item.title: 🇮🇷 US Intelligence: Iran No Closer to Nuclear Weapon Despite War
item.description: US intelligence agencies estimate Tehran remains about a year away from being able to build a nuclear weapon, despite weeks of fighting, suggesting the conflict has not significantly degraded Iran's nuclear timeline.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russian Attacks Kill 15 Ukrainian Civilians in Past Day
item.description: Russia launched 11 Iskander ballistic missiles and 164 Shahed drones in overnight attacks, killing 15 civilians and injuring at least 72 across Ukraine despite the declared ceasefire.

item.title: 🇫🇮 Finland Joins Ukraine-Led Corpus Defense Procurement Coalition
item.description: Finland has joined the Ukraine-led Corpus defense procurement coalition, aiming to strengthen defense procurement coordination and supply chain resilience among partner nations.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Hails Leap Forward in Ties With Russia's Ally Armenia
item.description: The EU and Armenia laid the groundwork for closer ties at a summit in Yerevan, marking another step in the former Soviet republic's pivot away from Moscow toward the West.

item.title: 🇷🇴 Romanian PM faces no-confidence vote as coalition collapses
item.description: Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan faces a parliamentary vote that could topple his pro-European government less than a year after taking office. The leftist Social Democratic Party joined forces with the hard-right AUR party to submit the motion.

item.title: 🛡️ US defense contractor Leidos raises 2026 forecast on strong military demand
item.description: Defense contractor Leidos Holdings raised its profit and revenue forecast for 2026, citing strong demand for IT and military intelligence services amid ongoing global conflicts and the Iran-US war.

item.title: 📰 Reuters wins two Pulitzer Prizes for Trump investigations and Meta exposé
item.description: Reuters won Pulitzer Prizes for national reporting on Trump's campaign of political retribution and for beat reporting exposing Meta's harmful AI and advertising practices.

item.title: 🚢 Second hantavirus case confirmed on cruise ship after three deaths
item.description: The WHO confirmed a second hantavirus case on the MV Hondius cruise ship anchored off Cape Verde. Three passengers have died and WHO warns human-to-human transmission may be occurring among close contacts.

item.title: 🇸🇩 Sudan Accuses Ethiopia of Drone Attacks, Recalls Ambassador
item.description: Sudan military accuses Ethiopia and UAE of drone strikes on Khartoum, recalling its ambassador from Addis Ababa as regional tensions escalate.
